spec_match.rationale: The page now uses a real audio element backed by a throttled service-worker WAV stream, with authored CSS selectors for audio:buffering, audio:seeking, and audio:stalled rather than JS-toggled mock classes.

interactive.rationale: Controls load slow or stalled media streams, play and pause the element, seek near the end, stress buffering with playbackRate, and mute while live media readouts and event logs update.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still demonstrates the network-dependent pseudo-classes promised by the feature blurb, but now does so with actual media loading and seeking behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers unsupported selector parsing, missing service worker fallback, slow buffering, a deterministic stalled stream, real seeking, and native event/readiness diagnostics.

references.rationale: The page links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5068277495758848 and to the HTML Standard media pseudo-class section.

design_system.rationale: Local styling uses shared CSS variables for colors, button background and color are set together, and the concept is a working media lab rather than a static card.

summary: The major critique is resolved: the buffering-states concept no longer mocks platform state and instead drives a real media element through slow, stalled, and seeking scenarios. Local Chrome Canary 150.0.7871.2 still did not parse the new selectors, so the tested path verified the graceful fallback plus native media events rather than live selector matching.
